problem: The continuation context (takes priority) requires making CLI tokens org-scoped with local multi-token storage. Currently the CLI stores a single apiKey + optional orgId/orgName. The continuation context requires: (1) new 'hlx token add' command that validates a token with the server and stores it under the key's actual org, (2) multi-org token entries in ~/.hlx/config.json with current-org pointer, (3) 'hlx org list' must be local-only (currently calls /api/auth/me), (4) 'hlx org switch' must switch which stored token is current (not just update orgId), (5) env var token override must continue working for Helix agents.

files.why: Core config module (82 lines). HxConfig type (L12-19) has single apiKey, url, optional orgId/orgName, autoUpdate, installSource. loadConfig() (L24-49): env vars (HELIX_API_KEY > HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN > HELIX_INSPECT_API_KEY for token; HELIX_URL > HELIX_INSPECT_BASE_URL > HELIX_INSPECT_URL for URL) take priority; falls back to config.json. saveConfig() (L62-73): read-merge-write. Must change to support multi-token config shape: orgs array of { orgId, orgName, token, url, alias? } entries + currentOrg pointer.

HTTP client (134 lines). hxFetch() (L37-134): sets X-API-Key for hxi_ tokens (L53-54), Authorization: Bearer for others (L56). Already sends X-Helix-Org-ID when config.orgId is set AND token starts with hxi_ (L59-61). Under org-scoped model the token already represents its org; header may still be sent for server-side validation (must match key's org).
cmdOrgCurrent (L17-24): calls /api/auth/me and displays org name, ID, user name, email. Under multi-token model, current org is determined by which token entry is selected locally.

cmdOrgList (L10-26): currently calls /api/auth/me to get availableOrganizations from server. For hxi_ tokens, marks current by comparing org.id === config.orgId (L20-22). Continuation context requires this to be LOCAL-ONLY, reading configured org entries from config.json.

cmdOrgSwitch (L14-72): resolves org name via /api/auth/me for non-CUID input (L27-48). For hxi_ tokens does local-only switch: saveConfig({ orgId, orgName }) (L50-53). For JWT does server-side switch (L55-71). Under multi-token model, switch must change which stored token entry is current, and name resolution must use local config entries.
Org command dispatcher (35 lines). Routes current/list/switch subcommands. May need new subcommand routing or top-level 'token' command may be separate.
Main entry point (94 lines). Command dispatch switch (L49-90). Would need a new 'token' command case for 'hlx token add'. requireConfig() called for auth-needed commands.

Login flows (109 lines): OAuth browser flow (L49-108) and --manual paste (L31-41). Both save single token via saveConfig(). Under multi-token model, login may need adjustment or remain as a separate JWT-oriented flow.
CLI flag parsing utilities. Needed for implementing --token, --url, --name, --current flags on token add command.
Build config: strict mode, ES2022 target, Node16 module resolution, outDir: dist. No test config.

facts.claim: Current HxConfig type: { apiKey: string, url: string, orgId?: string, orgName?: string, autoUpdate?: boolean, installSource?: InstallSource }. Single token only.

loadConfig() env var priority: HELIX_API_KEY > HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN > HELIX_INSPECT_API_KEY for token; HELIX_URL > HELIX_INSPECT_BASE_URL > HELIX_INSPECT_URL for URL. When env vars used, orgId/orgName NOT loaded (returns { apiKey, url } only).
hxFetch already sends X-Helix-Org-ID header when config.orgId is set AND token starts with hxi_: headers['X-Helix-Org-ID'] = config.orgId.
hlx org list currently makes a server call to /api/auth/me and reads availableOrganizations. For hxi_ tokens marks current by config.orgId; for JWT by data.organization.id.
hlx org switch for hxi_ tokens already does local-only switch: saveConfig({ orgId: organizationId, orgName: orgName }). No server call.
hlx org switch for non-CUID names resolves org name via /api/auth/me availableOrganizations. Handles ambiguous names with error. Under local-only model this must use local config entries.
No 'hlx token add' command exists. Token management is via 'hlx login' (OAuth or --manual), both saving a single apiKey.
No test files exist in the CLI repo. No test script in package.json.
Quality gates: typecheck (tsc --noEmit), build (tsc). No test or lint commands.
requireConfig() exits with process.exit(1) and message: 'Not authenticated. Run hlx login <server-url> or set HELIX_API_KEY + HELIX_URL env vars.'
Config stored at ~/.hlx/config.json (CONFIG_DIR = ~/.hlx). saveConfig does read-merge-write with mkdirSync recursive.
Config file is also used to store autoUpdate and installSource metadata from the update system.

unknowns.question: What should the new multi-token config.json shape look like? Likely an 'orgs' array of { orgId, orgName, token, url, alias? } entries plus a 'currentOrg' pointer, with backward-compat fields (autoUpdate, installSource) preserved.
unknowns.impact: Config shape drives all CLI command changes. Must be decided in diagnosis.

unknowns.question: Should 'hlx token add' validate the token by calling /api/auth/me to learn the key's org? The continuation context says 'validate the token with the server, learn the token's actual org.'
unknowns.impact: Determines which server endpoint to call and what response shape to expect. Currently /api/auth/me requires requireAuth + requireOrgForApiKey which may block a new token without org header.

unknowns.question: How should 'hlx login' (OAuth and manual) interact with the new multi-token model? OAuth returns a JWT, not an hxi_ key. Manual login could receive an hxi_ key.
unknowns.impact: May need hlx login --manual to become an alias for hlx token add, or remain a separate flow for JWT-based auth.

unknowns.question: Should the CLI still send X-Helix-Org-ID header under org-scoped model? The server continuation context says 'if an org header is still accepted for compatibility, it must match the key's org exactly or fail closed.'
unknowns.impact: Determines whether http.ts org header logic stays, is removed, or sends the key's org for validation.

unknowns.question: How does token validation work for 'hlx token add' when the key has no org header set yet? The server currently requires X-Helix-Org-ID for API-key auth on /api/auth/me (behind requireOrgForApiKey). But under org-scoped model, the key always resolves its own org.
unknowns.impact: Chicken-and-egg: the CLI needs to validate the token before knowing the org, but the server might need the org header. Under org-scoped model this resolves naturally since the key always knows its org.
